_comment: Gate-set bundles for `haggis-eval slice <name>`. Each bundle is an ordered list of gate IDs that map to the wired subcommands in tools/haggis-eval/internal/cmd. Use these to run an opinionated subset without typing the gate matrix every time. Filename note: the kernel design spec calls this slices.toml; this project's haggis-eval CLI is stdlib-only Go, so JSON is the right call (encoding/json is stdlib; TOML is not). The schema field versions the config so future shape changes can be migrated without surprise.

pre-merge.description: Pre-merge gate-set — what CI runs on PRs. Skips the slower native rust workspace test, C-vs-Rust hash fuzz, WAT differential test, soak, and supply-chain audit, all of which are release-gate territory. Keeps cargo fmt + clippy, current docs/eval claim checks, typecheck + eslint + vitest + build + verify-dist, coverage thresholds, security headers, perf budgets, browser smokes, determinism, visual, and a11y.

release.description: Full release-gate matrix. Equivalent to `haggis-eval all` but invoked through the slice mechanism so a release script can name the bundle explicitly. Useful when you want the FNV-signed tamper-evident JSON report generated — note: only `all` writes the report; `slice release` runs the same gates but does NOT sign a report.

fast.description: Smoke-only — the absolute minimum to confirm the toolchain still works. Current docs/eval claim checks, typecheck, vitest, build, bundle-size + paint-timing budgets. ~10 seconds end to end. Use when iterating on docs or non-rendered code paths.
